LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TOP VISION DEVELOPMENT, LLC'S MOTION TO 1) WITHDRAW STIPULATION AND ORDER OF REFERENCE TO SPECIAL MASTER AND 2) TO SET ASIDE CASE MANAGEMENT ORDER NO. 1. A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANT DEMONSTRATES GOOD CAUSE TO WITHDRAW THE STIPULATION AND ORDER REGARDING THE APPOINTMENT OF A SPECIAL MASTER. DEFENDANT AGREED THAT MR. HENNINGSEN COULD SERVE AS SPECIAL MASTER AND HE IS NO LONGER AVAILABLE. THE COURT HAS NO AUTHORITY UNDER JOVINE V. FHP, INC. (1998) 64 CAL.APP.4TH 1506, 1522, TO IMPOSE A NEW SPECIAL MASTER ON THE PARTIES. THE COURT VACATES CMO1 BECAUSE IT IS LARGELY OUTDATED SINCE THERE IS NO LONGER A "SPECIAL MASTER" TO CONTROL THE LITIGATION. UNDER LU V. SUP. CRT. (1997) 55 CAL.APP.4TH 1264, THERE IS GOOD CAUSE TO APPOINT A DISCOVERY REFEREE TO MANAGE DISCOVERY DISPUTES IN THIS ACTION PER CCP 639(A)(5). PLAINTIFF IS BRINGING A COMPLEX $11 MILLION CONSTRUCTION DEFECT ACTION INVOLVING NUMEROUS PARTIES AND THE REFEREE CAN DEVELOP A DISCOVERY PLAN THAT REDUCES DUPLICATIVE AND CUMBERSOME DISCOVERY. THE COURT ENTERS A DISCOVERY STAY LIMITED IN DURATION UNTIL A REFEREE IS APPOINTED TO MANAGE THE DISCOVERY IN THIS CASE. THE COURT RECOMMENDS RETIRED COMM. BEST IF AVAILABLE. THE COURT CONTINUES THIS MOTION UNTIL JULY 1, 2015, TO GIVE THE PARTIES A CHANCE TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE SELECTION OF A DISCOVERY REFEREE. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302.

LAW AND MOTION 302, INTERVENOR AIG SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S JOINDER TO MOTION TO SET ASIDE CASE MANAGEMENT ORDER NO 1. A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANT DEMONSTRATES GOOD CAUSE TO WITHDRAW THE STIPULATION AND ORDER REGARDING THE APPOINTMENT OF A SPECIAL MASTER. DEFENDANT AGREED THAT MR. HENNINGSEN COULD SERVE AS SPECIAL MASTER AND HE IS NO LONGER AVAILABLE. THE COURT HAS NO AUTHORITY UNDER JOVINE V. FHP, INC. (1998) 64 CAL.APP.4TH 1506, 1522, TO IMPOSE A NEW SPECIAL MASTER ON THE PARTIES. THE COURT VACATES CMO1 BECAUSE IT IS LARGELY OUTDATED SINCE THERE IS NO LONGER A "SPECIAL MASTER" TO CONTROL THE LITIGATION. UNDER LU V. SUP. CRT. (1997) 55 CAL.APP.4TH 1264, THERE IS GOOD CAUSE TO APPOINT A DISCOVERY REFEREE TO MANAGE DISCOVERY DISPUTES IN THIS ACTION PER CCP 639(A)(5). PLAINTIFF IS BRINGING A COMPLEX $11 MILLION CONSTRUCTION DEFECT ACTION INVOLVING NUMEROUS PARTIES AND THE REFEREE CAN DEVELOP A DISCOVERY PLAN THAT REDUCES DUPLICATIVE AND CUMBERSOME DISCOVERY. THE COURT ENTERS A DISCOVERY STAY LIMITED IN DURATION UNTIL A REFEREE IS APPOINTED TO MANAGE THE DISCOVERY IN THIS CASE. THE COURT RECOMMENDS RETIRED COMM. BEST IF AVAILABLE. THE COURT CONTINUES THIS MOTION UNTIL JULY 1, 2015, TO GIVE THE PARTIES A CHANCE TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE SELECTION OF A DISCOVERY REFEREE. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302.

Notice Of Joinder By Defendants M M Lightweight Concrete Company, Nicodemus Plumbing And Milo Masonry To Top Vision Development, Llcs Motion To Set Aside Case Management Order No. 1
Off Calendar Jun-04-2015 Continued To Jul-01-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John P. Gill, of Hughes Gill Cochrane P.C., Tel. 925.926.1200, for plaintiffs;
Joseph C. Schultz, of Sellar Hazard & Lucia, Tel. 925.938.1430, for defendants Top Vision Development;
Liana Yung, of Ericksen Arbuthnot, Tel. 415.362.7126, for defendants Blue's Roofing Company, Davidson Plastering, Inc. and Tile West, Inc.;
James Patrick, of Ryan & Lifter, Tel. 925.884.2080 ext. 202, for defendants M & M Lightweight Concrete Company, Nicodemus Plumbing Co., and Milo Masonry, Inc., appearing via CourtCall;
Mark E. Petersen, of Weil & Drage, APC, Tel.949.837.8200, for defendants Casey-Fogli Concrete Contractors, Inc., Service Lathing Company, appearing via CourtCall;
Matthew Day, of Porter Scott, Tel. 916.929.1481, for intervenor AIG, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS NICODEMUS PLIMBING & MECHANICAL, MILO MASONRY, INC., M & M LIGHTWEIGHT CONCRETE COMPANY, INC.'S Joinder To Top Vision Development, LLC's Motion To Set Aside Case Management Order No. 1 is granted in part. Defendant demonstrates good cause to withdraw the stipulation and order regarding the appointment of a special master. Defendant agreed that Mr. Henningsen could serve as special master and he is no longer available. The court has no authority under Jovine v. FHP, Inc. (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 1506, 1522, to impose a new special master on the parties. The court vacates CMO1 because it is largely outdated since there is no longer a "special master" to control the litigation. Under Lu v. Sup. Crt. (1997) 55 Cal.App.4th 1264, there is good cause to appoint a discovery referee to manage discovery disputes in this action per CCP 639(a)(5). Plaintiff is bringing a complex $11 million construction defect action involving numerous parties and the referee can develop a discovery plan that reduces duplicative and cumbersome discovery. The court enters a discovery stay limited in duration until a referee is appointed to manage the discovery in this case. The court recommends retired Comm. Best if available. The court continues this motion until July 1, 2015, to give the parties a chance to meet and confer regarding the selection of a discovery referee. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
